title: Providence Park Opens in Southern Pulaski County as a New Approach to Homeless Housing

seo.title: Providence Park Homeless Housing Opens in Pulaski County | Metro Little Rock News
seo.description: A new residential community for people experiencing homelessness has opened in southern Pulaski County, tucked into a wooded area outside Little Rock.

summary: Providence Park, a new residential community designed to house people experiencing homelessness, has opened in the wooded terrain of southern Pulaski County. The facility represents an unconventional model for addressing homelessness in the Little Rock metro area. Its location, set back in brushy woods, keeps it out of plain sight but not out of service.

body_sections.body: A new community called Providence Park is now open in southern Pulaski County, offering housing to people experiencing homelessness in what organizers describe as a novel approach to a persistent regional problem. The facility is situated in a wooded, secluded area — an unlikely setting that nonetheless appears to be functioning as an operational residential site.

body_sections.heading: A Different Kind of Solution
body_sections.body: Rather than a traditional shelter model, Providence Park appears to be built around a more permanent or semi-permanent residential concept. Details on capacity, programming, and sponsoring organizations were not fully available at time of publication, but the opening marks a tangible step toward addressing homelessness outside of downtown Little Rock's existing shelter infrastructure.

impact_note: For residents and policymakers across the metro, Providence Park signals a willingness to test alternative models for housing instability — one worth watching as it matures.
